gen_load_yearly_summary: The generation mix in the German day-ahead electricity market over the trailing year had wind at 31.2%, solar at 17.0%, hydro and biomass at 11.1%, gas at 13.9%, and coal at 21.1%. Compared to the prior year, the share of wind increased and coal decreased. Over the last 7 days, solar was the dominant source at 38.2%, while in the same week a year ago, wind led at 25.2%. The imports and exports of electricity as a percentage of domestic generation were 15.69% and 11.96% over the trailing year, respectively, and 18.24% and 7.83% over the last 7 days.
price_yearly_summary: The mean price of electricity in the German day-ahead market over the trailing year was 93.32 EUR/MWh, which is higher than the 90.13 EUR/MWh in the prior year. The most expensive hour of the day was typically hour 19, with an average price of 141.67 EUR/MWh in the trailing year and 138.61 EUR/MWh in the prior year. The price was negative for 475 hours in the trailing year, compared to 622 hours in the prior year. Over the last 7 days, the mean price was 133.48 EUR/MWh, with the most expensive hour at 21 and an average price of 280.77 EUR/MWh, whereas in the same week a year ago, the mean price was 89.12 EUR/MWh.
